skill.name: employment-contract-templates
skill.description: Create employment contracts, offer letters, employee handbook sections, and HR policy drafts using structured templates and legal review reminders.
skill.summary: Draft structured employment documents with jurisdiction and counsel-review safeguards.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and hardcoded URLs. AI review found markdown formatting, ordinary employment-law prose, and documentation links only; no executable code, prompt injection, or exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks are markdown fences or inline formatting for template text and resource paths. They are not Ruby shell execution, do not run commands, and do not include user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are markdown code fences and inline file-path references. There is no executable Ruby, shell syntax, command invocation, or interpolation context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Legal Prose Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain frontmatter description and employment contract language, not cryptographic algorithms or security code. No hash, cipher, or password-storage logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is employment-document prose. I found no implementation code or cryptographic primitive use at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: HR Template Text Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe compensation, paid leave, holidays, conduct, and drafting guidance. They do not inspect the host system, enumerate files, read environment data, or collect machine metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are plain employment policy content. There is no command, API call, filesystem access, or host-information collection behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to SHRM, the Department of Labor, and EEOC guidance as human reference links. The skill does not automatically request these URLs or transmit user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are ordinary markdown links in a resources section. No network client, fetch call, tracking URL, or data exfiltration pattern is present.

content.user_title: Draft Employment Contracts and HR Documents
content.value_statement: Employment documents need clear terms and careful compliance review. This skill provides structured templates, clause checklists, and drafting prompts for common HR documents.
actual_capabilities: Drafts offer letters with role, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and acceptance sections.
Creates employment agreement drafts with confidentiality, IP, termination, and dispute terms.
Builds employee handbook policy sections for conduct, attendance, leave, security, and social media.
Prompts for jurisdiction, employment type, and required clauses before drafting.
Includes legal-review disclaimers and customization reminders for employment law differences.
limitations: Does not provide licensed legal advice or replace counsel review.
Does not verify current federal, state, provincial, or local employment law.
Does not determine enforceability of non-compete, arbitration, or wage clauses.
Requires accurate company, role, compensation, and jurisdiction inputs from the user.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Standard Offer Letter
use_cases.description: Create a first draft for a new employee with compensation, benefits, contingencies, and acceptance terms.

use_cases.title: Standardize HR Policy Sections
use_cases.description: Draft handbook language for attendance, PTO, harassment reporting, technology use, and workplace conduct.

use_cases.title: Review Contract Clause Coverage
use_cases.description: Check whether a draft includes confidentiality, IP assignment, termination, governing law, and acknowledgment sections.

prompt_templates.title: Draft an Offer Letter
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an offer letter for a [role] in [jurisdiction]. Include start date, compensation, benefits, contingencies, at-will language if applicable, and signature blocks.

prompt_templates.title: Create Handbook Policies
prompt_templates.prompt: Create employee handbook sections for [company type] covering equal employment opportunity, anti-harassment, work hours, PTO, sick leave, conduct, and technology use.

prompt_templates.title: Customize an Employment Agreement
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an employment agreement for a [full-time/part-time/contract] [role] in [jurisdiction]. Include compensation, benefits, confidentiality, IP assignment, termination, and governing law sections.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Clause Completeness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this employment document for missing clauses, unclear terms, and jurisdiction-sensitive provisions. Flag items that require legal counsel review before use: [paste draft].

output_examples.input: Need an offer letter for a remote software engineer in California.
output_examples.output: A structured offer letter with position details, salary, benefits, contingencies, at-will language, remote work terms, expiration date, and signature fields.

output_examples.input: Need handbook policies for PTO, harassment reporting, and acceptable technology use.
output_examples.output: Three concise policy sections with employee obligations, reporting steps, approval requirements, monitoring notice, and placeholders for company-specific thresholds.

output_examples.input: Need to check an employment agreement for missing terms.
output_examples.output: A clause checklist that identifies covered sections, missing items, ambiguous terms, and provisions that need jurisdiction-specific legal review.

best_practices: Confirm jurisdiction, employment classification, and worker status before drafting.
Replace all placeholders with company-specific details before review.
Have qualified counsel review final documents before signature or distribution.
anti_patterns: Using the same template across jurisdictions without local review.
Including restrictive covenants without checking current enforceability rules.
Treating generated text as final legal advice.

faq.question: Can this skill create a complete employment contract?
faq.answer: Yes, it can draft a structured first version. A qualified lawyer should review it before use.

faq.question: Does this skill provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides informational templates and drafting support, not legal advice.

faq.question: Can it handle different jurisdictions?
faq.answer: It prompts for jurisdiction and flags sensitive clauses. It does not verify current local law.

faq.question: What documents can it help draft?
faq.answer: It supports offer letters, employment agreements, handbook sections, HR policies, NDAs, and onboarding documents.

faq.question: Can it review an existing draft?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify missing clauses, unclear placeholders, and issues that need legal review.

faq.question: Is it suitable for sensitive employee data?
faq.answer: Use only the minimum necessary personal data. Remove confidential details unless they are required for drafting.
